heading: Subpoenas in multiparty, multiforum actions
label: 1785 Subpoenas in multiparty, multiforum actions
text: When the jurisdiction of the district court is based in whole or in part upon , a subpoena for attendance at a hearing or trial may, if authorized by the court upon motion for good cause shown, and upon such terms and conditions as the court may impose, be served at any place within the United States, or anywhere outside the United States if otherwise permitted by law.section 1369 of this title
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1785, , , provided a privilege against self-incrimination on examination under letters rogatory, prior to repeal by , , . See .act June 25, 1948, ch. 64662 Stat. 950Pub. L. 88–619, § 12(a)78 Stat. 998section 1782(a) of this titleOct. 3, 1964

Effective DateSection applicable to a civil action if the accident giving rise to the cause of action occurred on or after the 90th day after , see , set out as a note under .Nov. 2, 2002section 11020(c) of Pub. L. 107–273section 1369 of this title
